public_functions.behavior_description: Initializes a 3-phase complementary PWM on the GTM TOM1 Cluster_1 using the unified IfxGtm_Pwm driver with center-aligned mode at 20 kHz, 1 us rising/falling hardware dead time, and synchronous start/update. The function performs the following steps in this exact order: 1) Declare all configuration structures as locals: the main IfxGtm_Pwm_Config, an array of three IfxGtm_Pwm_ChannelConfig entries (logical channels 0..2), an array of three output pin configs (each with a complementary pin), an InterruptConfig instance for period notifications, and a persistent module-state struct (externally defined in the module) holding the Pwm handle, the persistent channels array (to be referenced by the driver), and arrays for dutyCycles[3] and deadTimes[3]. 2) Call IfxGtm_Pwm_initConfig to load default values into the main config. 3) Configure output[] for three logical channels: assign for each channel a high-side output pin and its complementary low-side pin (from the verified hardware macros), set output mode to push-pull and pad driver to cmosAutomotiveSpeed1, and set polarity active-high for both HS and LS. Configure DTM/dead-time in the config to 1.0 microsecond on both rising and falling edges. Set syncStart = TRUE and enable synchronous updates. Prepare InterruptConfig: set isrProvider=CPU0, priority=20, mode to generate period events, and set periodEvent to IfxGtm_periodEventFunction (empty stub). Assign the interruptConfig pointer to channelConfig[0].interrupt and NULL to channelConfig[1..2]. 4) Complete main config fields: select TOM1 Cluster_1, set alignment to center-aligned, set requested frequency to 20 kHz, and select FXCLK0 as the ATOM/TOM clock source in the config. 5) Inside a GTM enable guard: check IfxGtm_isEnabled; if disabled, enable the module with IfxGtm_enable, read the GTM module frequency dynamically via IfxGtm_Cmu_getModuleFrequency, configure GCLK and CLK0 to that frequency (conceptually; no hardcoded values), and enable FXCLK clocks via IfxGtm_Cmu_enableClocks. 6) Call IfxGtm_Pwm_init with the persistent channels array from the module state and the populated config to realize the 3-phase complementary PWM; the driver applies initial channel settings and routes the period interrupt per InterruptConfig. 7) Store initial duty cycle percentages into the module state as {U=25, V=50, W=75}. 8) Configure the LED/debug GPIO pin (P13.0) as a push-pull output using IfxPort_setPinModeOutput so the ISR can toggle it. Do not manually install interrupts or set up SRC; the high-level driver routes the interrupt internally using InterruptConfig. Do not call any STM functions or perform timing here.

Updates three phase duty cycles in percent (0..100) with a fixed STEP=10 and wrap rule: for each phase (U,V,W), compute next = current + STEP; if (current + STEP) >= 100 then set next = 0; then add STEP so the first update from 90 wraps to 0 then becomes 10. After updating the three percent values, convert them to normalized 0.0..1.0 fractions and place them into a 3-element array ordered by logical channel index 0..2. Call the unified PWM driver's immediate multi-channel duty update API to apply the new values coherently to all three complementary pairs with synchronous update semantics. No timing (STM) logic is included here; the caller is responsible for calling this periodically.

Empty period-event callback stub assigned through the PWM InterruptConfig. It is invoked by the driver's internal ISR routing on each PWM period event. Intentionally left empty; ISR toggling is handled by a separate ISR function.

header_selection.reconciliation_reason: LLM driver_calls use IfxGtm_Pwm_* API, not IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl (the reference API). Updated to match actual usage.
